description: Analytical reference cases for Lindblad solver validation. These have exact closed-form solutions and do NOT depend on QuTiP. Use for C fast-path validation.

cases.label: t1_decay_exact
cases.description: T1 decay of |1⟩ with no Hamiltonian. Analytical: ρ_11(t) = exp(-t/T1), ρ_00(t) = 1 - exp(-t/T1). Off-diagonals stay zero.
cases.derivation: ρ_11 = exp(-t/T1) = exp(-1e-4 / 50e-6) = exp(-2) ≈ 0.13534. T2=2*T1 so no pure dephasing (γ_φ=0).

cases.label: pure_dephasing_exact
cases.description: Pure dephasing of |+⟩ with no Hamiltonian. T2-only process: off-diagonals decay as exp(-t/T2), populations unchanged.
cases.derivation: T1 ~ infinity so γ1 ≈ 0. ρ_01(t) = ρ_01(0) * exp(-t/T2) = 0.5 * exp(-4e-5/2e-5) = 0.5 * exp(-2) ≈ 0.06767. Populations preserved exactly.

cases.label: ground_state_fixed_point
cases.description: Ground state |0⟩ is a fixed point of T1 + T2 dissipation. Output should equal input exactly.
cases.derivation: σ⁻|0⟩=0 and σz|0⟩=|0⟩, so all dissipator terms vanish for ρ=|0⟩⟨0|.

cases.label: rabi_oscillation_unitary
cases.description: Rabi oscillation with H = (Ω/2)σx, no dissipation. ρ_11(t) = sin²(Ωt/2). Ω chosen so Ωt = π (complete population inversion).
cases.hamiltonian_note: H = πf·σx where f = 12.5 GHz → Ω = 2πf = 25π GHz. Duration 20ns → Ωt = 25π * 20e-9 * 1e9 = 500π. ρ_11 = sin²(250π) = 0.0. Use hamiltonian_flat instead.
cases.derivation: Ω = π/(20ns) = π/20e-9 rad/s. H = (Ω/2)σx. After t=20ns: Ωt = π, so sin²(π/2 * n_full_cycles) returns to |0⟩. Purity preserved: Tr(ρ²) = 1.0.

cases.label: maximally_mixed_fixed_point
cases.description: Maximally mixed state I/2 is NOT a fixed point of T1 (it decays toward |0⟩), but IS invariant under pure dephasing. Test with T1 only.

cases.label: error_accumulation_1step_vs_1000
cases.description: Compare P^1000 (1000 applications of single-step propagator) vs computing result in one step. For time-independent Lindbladian, both should give identical results. Tests numerical stability of repeated matvec.
cases.derivation: For time-independent L, exp(L * T) = (exp(L * T/N))^N exactly in exact arithmetic. Difference measures floating-point error accumulation.

cases.label: d3_qutrit_t1_decay
cases.description: T1 decay of |2⟩ in a 3-level (qutrit) system. Tests d=3, the primary qutrit target. Collapse operators: L_10 = sqrt(γ_10)|1⟩⟨2|, L_01 = sqrt(γ_01)|0⟩⟨1|.
cases.note: Reference data should be generated from QuTiP mesolve with 3-level collapse operators. This is a placeholder structure; populate with QuTiP reference before writing C code.
